steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The first sentence states that scientists needed an accurate mathematical model of Earth's shape. The second sentence names Amelia Hart as the person who wrote the computer program to perform those calculations. The relationship is conclusion: Hart's program was the end result of the work described.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs a transition signaling that Hart's contribution was the final, culminating step of the process just described.
  • Choice A — Ultimately,: signals the final outcome of a process, fitting because Hart's program was the conclusive answer to the scientists' need for a mathematical model. ✓
  • Choice B — In other words,: signals a restatement of what was just said, but Hart's program is not a reworded version of the need for a model — it is the resolution of that need. ✗
  • Choice C — Secondly,: signals a second step in a series, but no first step was listed in the previous sentence — and Hart's work was the end of the process, not an intermediate step. ✗
  • Choice D — In addition,: signals supplementary information, but Hart's program is not merely an add-on — it is the conclusion that resolved the problem stated. ✗
A is correct.
